label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern cmp,vp,vpc,naive = W,L,W,L (cmplog AND value_profile_cmplog resolve; value_profile AND naive block) => i2s-PRO single-technique literal-substitution family. The evidence tests verify this independently (G2): they measure the cmplog corpus's literal CONTENT (byte-frequency enrichment at a fixed offset; offset-agnostic anchor-token presence vs depletion), or what the resolving corpus dynamically REACHES (per-seed branch-line depth + decisive-side-taken), or how CLOSE each arm's seed-set lands to the gate operand (value-distance residual) — NEVER the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: cmplog (I2S) substitutes a logged CMP literal at a fixed gate offset, so its corpus is enriched in the target value at that offset relative to naive; value_profile/naive cannot synthesize the exact literal

params.applies_to_signatures: operand_kind in {multibyte_literal, single_byte_constant, enum_or_code} AND byte_signature == contiguous_literal AND gate_structure in {equality, switch_case, presence_or_nonnull, length_or_count_check, inequality_or_range}
params.note: lcms/libpng/libxml2/bloaty have no on-disk corpus on s4 (data_realities); their owning server (sB) arbitrates them. Tool returns no_corpus on a server lacking the corpus -> that server leaves the branch inconclusive for this hypothesis, it is NOT decidable:false.

hypotheses.prediction: For a fixed-offset literal value gate, the cmplog corpus is ENRICHED in the target literal at the gate offset relative to naive: signed_target_enrich > 0 (i2s-PRO direction). A pure-coverage / value-profile-only mechanism predicts NO cmplog-specific enrichment at the operand offset (signed_target_enrich ~ 0), because without I2S operand substitution cmplog's corpus byte distribution at that offset would match naive's. This is the alternative the test rules out.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: signed_target_enrich <= 0 (cmplog NOT enriched, or depleted, in the target literal at the gate offset) refutes i2s-PRO substitution at this gate.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Measures corpus byte-frequency at the operand offset (a property of saved seeds), not whether vpc/cmplog resolved the branch. Independent of the Layer-1 resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: I2S splices one or more logged CMP anchor literals (URL scheme '://', 'HTTP/'/'HTTP/2', header names, protocol tokens 'pop3', chunk-type FOURCCs 'IDAT'/'sRGB'/'eXIf'/'sCAL'/'tIME', OpenType table tags 'GPOS'/'GSUB', the Mach-O magic, the switch comparand byte) somewhere into the input; the literal is PRESENT in the cmplog-resolving corpus but ABSENT (depleted to filler-noise) in the naive/value_profile-blocking corpus, because only I2S operand substitution can plant the exact bytes. The branch is the value-compare itself OR sits one hop downstream of the planted token (dispatch into a handler / scaffold whose downstream state controls the branch).
hypotheses.novelty_vs_refuted: Distinct from the prior refuted offset-anchored value_enrichment (forbidden on dispersed/different-sized W/L seeds). Uses OFFSET-AGNOSTIC, SIZE-TOLERANT per-seed occurrence COUNTING of named anchor literals with a winner-present / loser-depleted presence RATIO (token_count.py).

params.applies_to_signatures: branch has at least one recoverable anchor literal (signature.operand_literal is a printable/hex literal, OR the card names a concrete scaffold token), regardless of byte_signature and operand_kind.
params.multi_anchor_rule: when a branch lists >1 anchor literal, score each with token_count and take the MAX presence-depletion (best single recoverable anchor).
params.note: Cross-server: each server scores its own targets; a server lacking the corpus returns no_corpus -> inconclusive there (NOT decidable:false). token_count needs >=1 resolving + >=1 blocking seed for the branch.

hypotheses.prediction: If the branch is reached via an I2S-planted anchor literal, that literal is PRESENT in the cmplog-resolving seeds and DEPLETED in the naive/value_profile-blocking seeds: winner_literal_count >= 1 AND literal_presence_ratio << 1. The alternative this rules out is that value_profile's Hamming gradient (not I2S substitution) assembles the scaffold or the token is corpus-generic — either predicts comparable presence in both arms (literal_presence_ratio ~ 1).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: literal_presence_ratio ~ 1 (anchor literal equally present in the blocking seeds) OR winner_literal_count < 1 refutes I2S-driven anchor-literal scaffold assembly for this branch.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Reads per-seed occurrence counts of a named literal over the saved resolving vs blocking corpora; never inspects whether cmplog/vpc resolved. Independent of the Layer-1 resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: I2S assembles a MULTI-FIELD structure whose downstream effect is that the cmplog-RESOLVING corpus, run dynamically, DRIVES execution deeper to the blocker line AND TAKES the decisive side, while the naive/value_profile-BLOCKING corpus reaches the line only shallowly and never takes that side. Discriminator = ASSEMBLED-STRUCTURE EXECUTION DEPTH, not the presence of any single byte.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_refuted: Distinct measurement-CLASS from the static byte tools (A,B): RUNS the seeds and measures DYNAMIC REACH (depth_lift, side_lift). Re-aims the registry depth_reach tool's ctx/ngram iteration-depth subtype at the I2S structural-assembly subtype.

params.branch_id_form: use the BARE numeric branch_id; the '_h0' suffix is a hypothesis sub-id, not the DB branch key.
params.note: depth_reach is Docker per-branch and needs the libafl-<target>-cov image + resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds on-disk. Cov images exist for curl/harfbuzz (s4) and libpng/libxml2 (sB). A server lacking the corpus/image returns skip -> inconclusive there (NOT decidable:false). lcms + bloaty have NO cov image on either server. ~30-60s per branch.

hypotheses.prediction: If I2S unlocks the branch by assembling a multi-field structure, the cmplog-resolving corpus run dynamically REACHES the blocker line far more per seed AND TAKES the decisive side, while the naive-blocking corpus stalls: depth_lift >= 2 AND side_lift >= 3. The ALTERNATIVE ruled out: 'the loser corpus reaches the branch line equally deep, it just carries the wrong gate VALUE' (depth_lift ~ 1, side_lift ~ 1).
hypotheses.rule: depth_lift >= 2.0 AND side_lift >= 3.0 AND winner_deeper == true
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: depth_lift ~ 1 AND side_lift ~ 1 refutes the structural-assembly-reach mechanism (the divergence is then a pure value/byte mismatch the static or value-distance tools own, not an assembled-structure depth effect). winner_deeper == false also refutes.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Runs each ARM's CORPUS through the instrumented binary and reads the branch-line execution + decisive-side-taken counts; never reads the resolve label.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 3). The blocking arm (naive AND, critically, value_profile — which has the CMP_MAP feedback but NO operand-substitution mutator) REACHES the gate line just as deeply as the winner (often deeper: the cards show losers hitting the dispatcher/switch/predicate line many times — libxml2 L hits=390, bloaty FindContaining L=1720, lcms_2330 losers saturate the shallow arm), so the divergence is NOT a reach/depth effect (which is why depth_reach's depth_lift>=2 was refuted on these). Instead, the discriminator is VALUE PRECISION at the line: cmplog (I2S) directly SUBSTITUTES the exact logged operand, so the closest any cmplog-resolving seed gets to the gate operand is residual ~0 (the literal is present), while the closest any loser-blocking seed (naive's blind havoc, OR value_profile's Hamming gradient that can only approach a multi-byte literal it never lands) stalls at a measurable residual distance. The winner LANDS the operand; the losers APPROACH-BUT-MISS it even at equal reach.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_refuted: GENUINELY DIFFERENT mechanism AND a tool NOT YET USED on this shape (value_distance_reached, registry pivot). The three already-refuted tests asked: A 'is the literal enriched at ONE fixed offset' (refuted: dispersed multi-field placement misaligns the offset scan on different-sized W/L seeds); B 'is the literal PRESENT anywhere in the winner yet absent in the loser' (refuted: the FOURCC/scheme token also shows up as filler/near-miss in some blocking seeds, so a binary presence ratio does not separate); C/depth_reach 'does the winner REACH the line deeper' (refuted: the losers reach the line equally or MORE — they take the WRONG SIDE, not a shallower path). This hypothesis measures neither offset-frequency, nor binary presence, nor reach: it measures the per-arm MINIMUM RESIDUAL DISTANCE to the operand over sliding windows (value_distance_reached). A near-miss filler byte in a loser still registers a NONZERO residual, so the metric separates 'landed the exact operand' (winner, residual 0) from 'approached but missed' (loser, residual > 0) — exactly the gap the presence/offset/depth tests are blind to. It is the orthogonal value-PRECISION axis, not a relaxed-threshold re-test of A/B/C (forbidden).

params.value_source: signature.operand_literal (the exact recoverable gate operand, per branch)

bloaty_141.note: Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe; numeric mode tolerates that losers carry the WASM magic 00 61 73 6d at the SAME offset (token_count B was refuted there because both magics 'present'); a residual-distance to ce fa ed fe separates a landed Mach-O magic (winner ~0) from a WASM-magic miss (loser > 0).

libxml2_6381.caveat: width<=2 chance-alignment prone (tool flags it); arbiter should heed the caveat — if caveat present AND distance_gap small, treat as inconclusive rather than confirmed.

params.note: value_distance_reached reads on-disk resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds for the branch; curl/harfbuzz on s4, libpng/libxml2/bloaty on sB. A server lacking the seeds returns skip -> inconclusive there (NOT decidable:false). bloaty has on-disk corpus on sB (data_realities lists bloaty among sB targets). Short-operand branches (width<=2: libxml2_6381) carry the tool's caveat and must clear a STRICTER gap to count.

hypotheses.prediction: If I2S unlocks the branch by substituting the exact operand at a line both arms already reach, the cmplog/vpc winner lands residual ~0 (operand present) while the closest value_profile/naive loser stalls at a positive residual: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap > 0 AND cmp_min_distance is at/near 0 while the closest loser stays away (distance_closure_ratio >= 0.5). The ALTERNATIVE this rules out is the depth alternative already refuted by depth_reach ('the loser never reaches the line') AND a pure-reach alternative ('both arms land the operand equally, divergence is elsewhere') — the latter predicts distance_gap ~ 0 / winner_closer == false. A clean winner-lands / loser-approaches-but-misses gap, AT a line both arms reach, is the value-precision signature the offset/presence/depth tools cannot see. Note value_profile being a LOSER here (it has the gradient feedback yet still stalls at distance) is itself the discriminating evidence that the gradient alone cannot place the multi-byte operand — only I2S substitution can.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap > 0 AND distance_closure_ratio >= 0.5
hypotheses.short_operand_rule: for branches flagged caveat 'short operand (width<=2)' (libxml2_6381): require the stricter distance_closure_ratio >= 0.75 AND distance_gap > 0 AND winner_closer == true; if not met, inconclusive (do not force-label on a width-1 chance alignment).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: distance_gap <= 0 OR winner_closer == false (the loser arm lands a seed at least as CLOSE to the operand as the winner) refutes I2S operand-value-precision for this branch — the divergence would then NOT be operand precision (it would be reach/depth, already refuted, or something no built tool sees).
hypotheses.g2_independence: Reads the MINIMUM residual distance each arm's saved seeds reached toward the gate operand (a property of what the seeds CONTAIN), never the resolve label. value_profile is deliberately scored as a LOSER, so the test cannot 'confirm cmplog-resolves with cmplog-resolves' — it asks whether the gradient-feedback arm ALSO stalls at distance. Independent of the Layer-1 W,L,W,L pattern.

hypotheses.label: HONEST decidable:false. The blocked branch is a DERIVED-state / structural-chain condition with (a) NO recoverable byte operand (so neither operand_enrichment, token_count, NOR value_distance_reached has a target value to measure) AND (b) on a target with NO libafl-<target>-cov image (so depth_reach cannot run). The gate is a runtime-derived quantity (a tone-curve float magnitude over 131072.0; an accrued bytecount being nonzero) or a deep multi-FOURCC NULL-guard chain whose discriminator is path-depth, not any single value. I2S still unlocks these upstream, but no BUILT measurement reaches them.

why_no_built_tool.lcms_2226: operand_literal null; gate is a RUNTIME tone-curve float magnitude (y >= 131072.0), no byte target for any value/presence/offset tool; lcms has NO cov image on either server so depth_reach is image-blocked. Both axes blocked.
why_no_built_tool.lcms_2330: operand_literal null; gate is a deep LUT-A2B NULL guard reached only by TRAVERSING a multi-FOURCC chain (the discriminator is path-depth, not one literal); the loser SATURATES the shallow Type_Curve default arm (reaches the line MORE), so even a value tool has no single operand and depth is image-blocked (no lcms cov image). Both axes blocked.
why_no_built_tool.curl_422: operand_kind derived_integer, operand_literal null; gate is k->bytecount nonzero on re-entry (a RUNTIME-accrued quantity, not a byte target), so value_distance_reached has no operand; the upstream scaffold is the dispersed URL/HTTP tokens already tested by B (refuted: tokens appear as filler in losers) and by depth_reach (refuted: equal reach). No remaining built tool measures a derived-integer accrual.
why_no_built_tool.curl_470: operand_kind derived_integer, operand_literal null; gate is bytecount+headerbytecount nonzero (same derived-accrual class as curl_422). No byte operand for value_distance_reached; B and depth_reach already refuted. No built tool reaches a derived-accrual gate.

params.what_would_make_it_decidable: lcms_2226/2330: building libafl-lcms-cov would let depth_reach probe their decisive-side reach (cards report W:T>0 vs L:F=0 / L hits=0 in deep arm). curl_422/470: a NEW 'derived-quantity reached' tool (run each arm's seeds, read the accrued runtime quantity at the gate, e.g. bytecount, from instrumentation) would discriminate — no such tool is built. Until then, honest inconclusive per G3 rather than force-labeled.

hypotheses.prediction: (undecidable with built tools on these branches) If a derived-quantity tool or the missing lcms cov image existed, it would show the cmplog-resolving corpus driving these branches' decisive side (over-magnitude false side; nonzero bytecount false side; deep LUT NULL guard) with the loser stalling on the shallow/other arm. No such measurement is available here.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: (no built measurement on these branches) — honest inconclusive per G3.

data_realities_respected: I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -> no seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence 'I2S event' descriptor proposed anywhere.

value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a feedback not a mutator -> its effect is read only via corpus content (byte-freq, token presence), what the corpus REACHES (depth_reach), or how CLOSE it lands to an operand (value_distance_reached, where vp is scored as a LOSER precisely because the gradient cannot PLACE a multi-byte literal). Never a lineage gradient event.

joint/structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED W vs L seeds -> the NEW value_distance_reached hypothesis uses a per-arm MINIMUM sliding-window residual (not a fixed-offset scan), so dispersed placement + size mismatch are handled by the window search + per-arm min, never the byte-offset alignment data_realities forbid on different-sized seeds.

depth_reach + value_distance_reached cov/seed needs: depth_reach needs the libafl-<target>-cov image (curl/harfbuzz/libpng/libxml2/openthread, NOT lcms/bloaty); value_distance_reached needs on-disk resolving+blocking seeds (curl/harfbuzz s4; libpng/libxml2/bloaty sB). The 4 decidable:false branches are lcms (no cov image, no operand) and the two curl derived-integer accrual gates (no byte operand for any value tool).

on-disk corpora exist only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (s4) and lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty (sB); each server scores its own targets and a missing corpus/image yields inconclusive (NOT decidable:false) — cross-server rule honored. bloaty_141 is value_distance_reached-scorable on sB (bloaty has on-disk corpus there).
seed-dependent tools (operand_enrichment, token_count, depth_reach, value_distance_reached) need resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite for the branch (the reps have them).

kept_priors: i2s_fixed_offset_literal_value_gate (A) — KEPT VERBATIM, FROZEN. Confirmed 73 branches via operand_enrichment (signed_target_enrich >= 0.8); re-validates them unchanged.

i2s_planted_anchor_literal_presence_scaffold_gate (B) — KEPT VERBATIM, FROZEN. Confirmed 10 branches via token_count (winner_literal_count >= 1 AND literal_presence_ratio <= 0.34); re-validates them unchanged.

i2s_structural_assembly_reach_depth_gate (C) — KEPT VERBATIM, FROZEN. Confirmed 2 branches via depth_reach (depth_lift>=2 AND side_lift>=3 AND winner_deeper). The residual-21 branches that did NOT clear depth_reach are NOT re-tested with a relaxed depth threshold — they are routed to the NEW value-precision hypothesis below (a categorically different measurement) or to decidable:false.
new_mechanism_for_unlabeled: i2s_operand_value_precision_at_equal_reach_gate (NEW, round 3) — the genuinely-different mechanism for 11 of the 21 residual branches, using the BUILT-but-not-yet-used-on-this-shape value_distance_reached.py (registry pivot). WHY the prior 3 tools all failed and this is novel: the cards show the losers REACH the gate line equally or MORE (libxml2 L hits=390; bloaty FindContaining L=1720; lcms_2330 losers saturate the shallow arm), so the divergence is NOT depth (depth_reach refuted), the operand is DISPERSED across fields (operand_enrichment's offset scan refuted), and the token also appears as filler/near-miss in losers (token_count's binary presence refuted). value_distance_reached measures the per-arm MINIMUM RESIDUAL to the operand: winner (cmplog/vpc) lands residual ~0 (I2S substitutes the exact operand) while the closest loser (naive blind havoc, AND value_profile's gradient that approaches-but-never-lands a multi-byte literal) stalls at a positive residual. Scoring value_profile as a LOSER is the crux — it has the CMP_MAP feedback yet still misses, proving the gradient alone cannot place the operand. Decidable members: libpng (3896_h0,3980_h0,4006,4031,4039_h0), harfbuzz_5560, curl_567, curl_129_h0, curl_322, bloaty_141 (numeric mode vs the WASM-magic near-miss), libxml2_6381 (width-1, stricter rule). Rule: winner_closer AND distance_gap>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5 (>=0.75 for the width<=2 short operand).
decidable_false_subgroup: i2s_derived_state_no_operand_no_cov_image (decidable:false) — HONEST inconclusive for exactly 4 branches that resist ALL FOUR built tools: lcms_2226 (runtime tone-curve float-magnitude gate, no byte operand, no lcms cov image), lcms_2330 (deep multi-FOURCC NULL-guard chain, no single operand, no lcms cov image, losers reach the shallow arm MORE), curl_422 + curl_470 (derived_integer bytecount-accrual gates — no byte operand for any value/presence/offset tool; B and depth_reach already refuted; a derived-quantity-reached tool would be needed but none is built). Per G3 they stay inconclusive rather than be force-labeled. Concrete decidability paths: build libafl-lcms-cov (moves lcms_2226/2330 under depth_reach) or build a 'derived-quantity reached' instrumentation tool (moves curl_422/470).
notes.round3_residual_disposition: 21 residual unlabeled branches: 11 -> NEW value_distance_reached value-precision hypothesis (libpng 3896_h0/3980_h0/4006/4031/4039_h0, harfbuzz_5560, curl_567/129_h0/322, bloaty_141, libxml2_6381); 6 curl URL-scaffold branches with NO clean operand and a derived/structural gate (curl_19, curl_22, curl_23, curl_298, curl_73) PLUS the 2 lcms + 2 curl derived branches -> see below. Of these, curl_19/22/23/298/73 are presence/state_or_grammar gates whose operand is the dispersed '://'/None scheme scaffold; their cards' discriminator is 'short noise loser vs 131-byte structured winner' (a SEED-SET SIZE/structure difference). The only built per-branch seed tool that could touch them is value_distance_reached on the '://' separator (width 3, dispersed) — but '://' is a 3-byte high-frequency punctuation run that also appears in loser filler ('%'/'+'/'.' runs include '/' and ':'), so a residual-distance test on '://' is chance-alignment-prone (it would near-align in losers), and there is no clean longer operand (no 'HTTP/'/'pop3' in the signature for these five). corpus_size_ratio (the other unused built tool) measures whole-corpus size/entropy and is BRANCH-INDEPENDENT (same number for every branch of the arm-pair), so it cannot per-branch-discriminate one curl scaffold gate from another and would violate G2 specificity. Therefore curl_19/22/23/298/73 have NO built tool that cleanly + per-branch-specifically discriminates their mechanism: they fall to the shape fallback 'inconclusive' (not a forced decidable:false hypothesis, because the OWNING server may re-bisect cleaner-operand seeds; per the cross-server rule they are left inconclusive for re-arbitration, consistent with the existing 'Re-bisect 3 s4 curl seed_starved branches' follow-up).
notes.discrimination_summary: Five-tier decision: (1) fixed-offset byte-freq enrichment (frozen A) [73 confirmed]; (2) offset-agnostic anchor-token presence-depletion (frozen B) [10 confirmed]; (3) dynamic structural-assembly execution-depth (frozen C, depth_reach) [2 confirmed]; (4) NEW operand value-precision at equal reach (value_distance_reached) for the 11 residual branches where losers REACH the line but APPROACH-BUT-MISS the operand; (5) honest decidable:false for the 4 derived-state/no-cov-image branches no built tool reaches. The remaining 6 curl '://'-scaffold branches (curl_19/22/23/298/73) carry NO clean per-branch operand and no per-branch-specific built tool -> shape fallback inconclusive, flagged for owning-server re-bisection. Tiers are distinguished by MEASUREMENT CLASS (byte-freq / token-presence / execution-depth / value-distance), so the data separates them; the inconclusive sets are non-discriminable by any BUILT tool on their branches.
